title: Bonannia graeca
intro: Bonannia graeca, also known as Greek Bonannia, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Apiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to support digestive health and reduce stress levels. The bioactive constituents of Bonannia graeca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, anxiety, and insomnia.
taxonomy: Bonannia graeca is classified within the family Apiaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It belongs to the subfamily Apioideae, which includes various plants with umbrella-shaped inflorescences and compound or simple leaves. Within the Apioideae subfamily, Bonannia graeca is placed in the tribe Scandiceae. The tribe Scandiceae is further divided into several genera, with Bonannia being one of the least studied. This genus, Bonannia, is endemic to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its unique appearance and distribution.
uses: Bonannia graeca, also known as Greek valerian or sweet valerian, is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues.
